steps.assistant_text: Three-stage sub-object finalize/validate (kernel, 0xF8 / 62 insns). Sibling of the MATCHED func_80001CF4, with an extra threaded arg. Disassembled target from expected/src/kernel/kernel_000.c.o. For each non-NULL handle slot (0x54/0x50/0x4C) the target calls the 5-arg helper func_80001CF0(self, handle, paramA, paramB, arg1): self=a0 (held in s0 from `move s0,a0`), handle=a1, paramA=a2, paramB=a3, and the 5th arg (arg1) is saved at sp+0x2c in the prologue then reloaded into t6/t7/t8 and stored to the sp+0x10 stack-arg slot in each call's delay region. func_80000518(handle, paramC) runs ALWAYS (commit emitted in the `beql s1,0,next` branch-likely delay slot, i.e. BEFORE the failure test); then temp_s1 is tested and -0xE/-0xF/-0x10 returned on failure. The prior NM body used a 4-arg call + return-before-commit shape (~63%); the correct form is the CF4 commit-before-check structure with self as the leading 5th-removed... i.e. 5-arg call. The memo 'adding a leading s arg makes it 5-arg and regresses' was measured against the wrong control structure and does not apply. build/.o vs expected/.o func region 0x1dd0..0x1ec8: 248 bytes, byte-identical; reloc-set MATCH (func_80001CF0 x3, func_80000518 x3). Full `make clean && make` ROM byte-identical to baserom (cmp clean, verify OK).
